bullets.text: Q3 combined ratio was 94.2, within the target zone; net income was $232 million, down 16% year-over-year due to investment timing differences, while year-to-date comprehensive income was essentially flat with last year.
Both Agency and Direct channels logged double-digit new application growth in the final month of Q3, signaling a recovery in unit growth after rate-driven deceleration throughout most of the year.
Written premium grew 5% for the quarter; premium growth remained rate-led, but new application acceleration indicated early-stage unit momentum building into Q4.

Rate adjustments in Florida auto proved highly productive, generating meaningful growth and strong margins; management reiterated state-by-state, market-by-market approach with small incremental changes.
Investment portfolio total return rebounded to 3.5% year-to-date after Q2 weakness; $54 million of 6.70% Debentures were repurchased, and $150 million of debt was retired at maturity on October 1st.
Snapshot reached a pace to factor into over $2 billion of 2013 personal auto written premium; four Snapshot intellectual property license arrangements were secured for use beginning in 2015.

New auto product design with latest underwriting features now represented the majority of countrywide offerings; Deductible Savings Bank and greater payment stability at renewal were prioritized in future designs.

Three new Service Centers opened in Omaha, Boston, and Dayton, bringing total to 61; Service Centers handled 'Cars not Cash' physical damage strategy and represented over a third of physical damage claims in served areas.

A new brand dimension was introduced beyond Flo's Superstore, attempting to reveal Progressive's internal values and culture to consumers; J.D. Power survey affirmed consumer comfort with multi-product bundling under a strong auto brand.

Multi-product penetration percentage continued to rise with auto-home bundling driving retention; one of eleven homeowner providers announced a change in ownership but with minimal expected impact on operations.
